author: Heinrich Neuhaus
title: The Art of Piano Playing
content: The teacher must develop the student's independent thinking, not mechanical obedience. The goal is to transform the notation into living music, not to reproduce symbols.

author: Royal Conservatory of Music
title: Graded Examination Syllabus
content: Students must demonstrate technical proficiency through standardized repertoire at each grade level. Assessment criteria emphasize accuracy, tempo, and adherence to score markings.

author: Seymour Bernstein
title: With Your Own Two Hands
content: Self-discovery is the key to musical growth. The practice room should be a laboratory, not a punishment chamber. Students must learn to listen to themselves with curiosity, not judgment.

author: Noa Kageyama
title: Performance Anxiety and the Musician's Brain
content: Performance anxiety activates the fight-or-flight response, fundamentally altering motor control and cognitive processing. Traditional performance preparation ignores neuroscience entirely.

author: Tobias Matthay
title: The Act of Touch in All Its Diversity
content: The arm and hand must be understood as a weight-bearing system. Tension is the enemy of tone. All problems of piano technique are ultimately problems of muscular coordination and release.

author: ABRSM
title: Music Theory Grades Syllabus
content: Theory must precede practice. Students should demonstrate written knowledge of scales, intervals, and harmonic progressions as prerequisite to advanced performance examinations.

author: Glenn Gould
title: Payzant Interview, 1974
content: The concert is not the culmination of musical study — it is its corruption. The truly musical experience is intimate, private, and freed from the social ritual of performance.

author: William Westney
title: The Perfect Wrong Note
content: Wrong notes are not failures — they are the most informative events in practice. A student who never makes mistakes is a student who never takes the risks necessary for growth.
